OOP & Java.question: Which OOP concept is also known as "Data Hiding"?
OOP & Java.explanation: Encapsulation bundles data and methods, restricting direct access — hence data hiding.

OOP & Java.question: What is the output of: System.out.println(10 + 20 + "30");
Which keyword prevents method overriding in Java?
OOP & Java.explanation: A final method cannot be overridden by subclasses.

OOP & Java.question: What is the default value of a boolean variable in Java?
OOP & Java.explanation: Default value of boolean is false.

OOP & Java.question: Which collection class maintains insertion order?
What is method overloading?
options: Different method name, same return type
OOP & Java.explanation: Overloading = same name but different number/type of parameters.

OOP & Java.question: What is a constructor?
options: Method called automatically when object is created
OOP & Java.explanation: Constructors initialize objects automatically on creation.

DBMS & SQL.question: Which SQL command is used to remove a table from a database?
DBMS & SQL.explanation: DROP TABLE removes the entire table structure and data.

DBMS & SQL.question: What is the difference between DELETE and TRUNCATE?
options: DELETE is DML, TRUNCATE is DDL; DELETE can be rolled back
TRUNCATE is DML; DELETE is DDL
DBMS & SQL.explanation: DELETE is DML (can be rolled back), TRUNCATE is DDL (cannot be rolled back).

DBMS & SQL.question: Which SQL clause is used to filter groups?
DBMS & SQL.explanation: HAVING filters records after GROUP BY aggregation.

DBMS & SQL.question: What does ACID stand for?
DBMS & SQL.explanation: ACID properties ensure database transaction reliability.

DBMS & SQL.question: What is a PRIMARY KEY?
options: Unique identifier for a record that cannot be NULL
DBMS & SQL.explanation: PRIMARY KEY uniquely identifies each row and cannot be NULL.

DBMS & SQL.question: What is normalization?
options: Process of organizing data to reduce redundancy
Process of adding redundancy for backup
DBMS & SQL.explanation: Normalization reduces data redundancy and improves data integrity.

DBMS & SQL.question: Which JOIN returns all records from both tables even if no match?
DBMS & SQL.explanation: FULL OUTER JOIN returns all rows from both tables.

DBMS & SQL.question: What is an index in a database?
options: Data structure that improves speed of retrieval
DBMS & SQL.explanation: Indexes speed up read queries at the cost of write overhead.

Operating Systems.question: What is a deadlock?
options: A state where two processes wait for each other indefinitely
Operating Systems.explanation: Deadlock: circular wait where processes block each other.

Operating Systems.question: What is virtual memory?
options: Disk space used to extend RAM
Operating Systems.explanation: Virtual memory uses disk space as an extension of RAM.

Operating Systems.question: Which scheduling algorithm can cause starvation?
Operating Systems.explanation: Priority Scheduling can starve low-priority processes indefinitely.

Operating Systems.question: What is the purpose of semaphores?
Operating Systems.explanation: Semaphores coordinate access to shared resources.

Operating Systems.question: What is thrashing?
Operating Systems.explanation: Thrashing: system spends more time swapping pages than executing.

Operating Systems.question: What is a process vs a thread?
options: Process = independent program, Thread = lightweight unit within process
Thread = independent program, Process = unit within thread
Operating Systems.explanation: Threads share memory within a process; processes are isolated.

Operating Systems.question: Which page replacement algorithm is optimal?
Operating Systems.explanation: OPT replaces the page not used for the longest time in future.

Operating Systems.question: What is the difference between preemptive and non-preemptive scheduling?
options: Preemptive can interrupt running process; non-preemptive cannot
Preemptive uses priority; non-preemptive uses time quantum
Operating Systems.explanation: Preemptive OS can forcibly take CPU away from a process.

Computer Networks.question: What does DNS stand for and what does it do?
options: Domain Name System — translates domain names to IP addresses
Computer Networks.explanation: DNS resolves human-readable domain names to IP addresses.

Computer Networks.question: What is the difference between TCP and UDP?
options: TCP is reliable/connection-oriented; UDP is faster/connectionless
UDP is reliable; TCP is not
Computer Networks.explanation: TCP ensures delivery with acknowledgements. UDP is faster but unreliable.

Computer Networks.question: Which layer does IP operate at in the OSI model?
Computer Networks.explanation: IP operates at Layer 3 — the Network Layer.

Computer Networks.question: What is a subnet mask?
options: Identifies which part of IP is network vs host
Computer Networks.explanation: Subnet mask divides IP address into network and host portions.

Computer Networks.question: What is the purpose of HTTP and HTTPS?
options: Protocol for web communication; HTTPS adds SSL encryption
Computer Networks.explanation: HTTPS uses TLS/SSL to encrypt data in transit.

Computer Networks.question: What is a MAC address?
options: Unique hardware identifier assigned to a network interface
Computer Networks.explanation: MAC addresses are Layer 2 physical addresses burned into network cards.

Computer Networks.question: What does ARP do?
options: Resolves IP address to MAC address
Computer Networks.explanation: ARP (Address Resolution Protocol) maps IP → MAC addresses.

Computer Networks.question: What is a firewall?
options: Network security system that monitors and controls incoming/outgoing traffic
Computer Networks.explanation: Firewalls filter traffic based on predefined security rules.

Data Structures.question: What is the time complexity of binary search?
Data Structures.explanation: Binary search halves search space each step: O(log n).

Data Structures.question: Which data structure uses LIFO principle?
What is the time complexity of inserting into a Hash Map on average?
Data Structures.explanation: HashMap uses hashing for O(1) average insertion.

Data Structures.question: What is a balanced binary search tree?
options: BST where height difference of left/right subtrees is at most 1
A BST with all nodes having 2 children
Data Structures.explanation: AVL trees and Red-Black trees maintain balance for O(log n) ops.

Data Structures.question: What is the worst case time complexity of Quick Sort?
Data Structures.explanation: Quick sort worst case is O(n²) when pivot is always min/max.

Data Structures.question: Which data structure is best for implementing BFS?
Data Structures.explanation: BFS uses Queue (FIFO) to process nodes level by level.

Data Structures.question: What is dynamic programming?
options: Breaking complex problems into overlapping subproblems and storing results
Data Structures.explanation: DP = memoization/tabulation to avoid redundant computations.

Data Structures.question: What is a Trie data structure used for?
options: Efficient string searching and prefix matching
Data Structures.explanation: Tries efficiently store strings for prefix/autocomplete searches.
